refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (on-site vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (teleoperation, safety services, vehicle control) and anchors verification to measurable, time-bound operational data.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week). Log task completion (volume moved, tonnage), elapsed time, and safety incidents. Compare achieved productivity against productivity_target. Validate that all safety alerts and operational policies were honored during measurement window.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and payload capacity.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote operator to command and monitor vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient for safe, productive task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and prevent unsafe task sequences.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The solution shall measure and report productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h) in real-time and post-operation.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-capacity vehicle with optimized hydraulic cycle, coupled with real-time telemetry and safety interlock system; measure productivity via onboard sensors and cloud analytics.
DP-B: Deploy vehicle with baseline hydraulic system; add teleoperation capability with safety services; measure productivity via manual operator logs and post-shift analysis.

DP-C: Implement modular vehicle architecture with swappable work tools; enable both on-site and remote operation modes; integrate safety services as middleware; measure productivity via integrated telemetry and AI-driven optimization.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Integrate high-capacity vehicle with optimized hydraulic cycle, coupled with real-time telemetry and safety interlock system; measure productivity via onboard sensors and cloud analytics.

potential_sources: Vehicle hydraulic cycle optimization is independent of teleoperation mode (on-site or remote operator can command the same optimized cycle).
Real-time telemetry and safety interlocks are decoupled: telemetry feeds productivity measurement; interlocks enforce safety constraints orthogonally.
Cloud analytics for productivity measurement does not depend on specific operator interface (on-site vs. remote).
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling because the three sub-solutions (vehicle optimization, telemetry, safety interlocks) address distinct functional concerns and can be developed and tested independently. Telemetry and safety services share the Network Link interface but do not create functional coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Onboard sensors (flow meters, pressure transducers, GNSS, load cells) are sufficiently accurate and reliable to measure productivity within ±5% over an 8-hour shift.
Assumption: Cloud analytics pipeline can ingest, process, and report productivity metrics with <5 min latency.
Risk: Sensor calibration drift over time may degrade productivity measurement accuracy; mitigation: implement periodic calibration protocol and sensor health monitoring.
Risk: Network outages may interrupt telemetry; mitigation: implement local buffering and post-sync when connectivity restored.
Risk: Safety interlocks may reduce achievable cycle time if overly conservative; mitigation: tune interlock thresholds via field trials.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: None at this stage. DP-A is well-scoped and decoupled. Proceed to IM assignment and sub-FR decomposition.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution IM is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. It owns the end-to-end productivity KPI delivery, including onboard sensor integration, telemetry aggregation, safety policy enforcement, and cloud analytics for productivity reporting.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h), vehicle state (position, orientation, payload), and sensor health status to external analytics or customer dashboard.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task commands (dig location, load target, dump zone) from Site Manager or Tele-operator via Teleoperation module.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits safety alerts (geofence breach, stability warning, interlock activation) to Safety Manager and on-site personnel.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution maintains network connectivity for telemetry uplink, tasking downlink, and safety alert distribution.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for CR-NGHE-001. It owns the productivity KPI and integrates all sub-systems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary.

Vehicle module owns the hydraulic cycle and onboard sensors, which are core to productivity. However, productivity measurement logic (analytics, KPI calculation) resides in NGHE Solution or cloud backend, not in Vehicle. Significant diffusion: productivity KPI is split between Vehicle (sensor data generation) and NGHE Solution (aggregation and reporting).

Teleoperation module owns operator interface and command transmission, but does not own vehicle control logic, sensor integration, or productivity measurement. High diffusion: productivity KPI depends on Vehicle and Safety Services, not Teleoperation.

Safety Services owns safety interlocks and policy enforcement, which constrain productivity (e.g., reduced cycle time due to safety checks). However, productivity measurement and optimization are not Safety Services responsibilities. Moderate diffusion: Safety Services influences but does not own the productivity KPI.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary because it is the only module that can own the end-to-end productivity KPI. The Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules are sub-systems that contribute to productivity but do not independently measure or report it. NGHE Solution integrates these sub-systems and owns the productivity measurement, analytics, and reporting logic.
containment_risks: Risk: If productivity analytics are implemented in a cloud backend (external to NGHE Solution), the IM boundary may be unclear. Mitigation: clarify whether cloud analytics are part of NGHE Solution or a separate external system; if external, define Telemetry interface contract precisely.

Risk: If Vehicle module owns some productivity optimization logic (e.g., cycle time tuning), there may be diffusion between Vehicle and NGHE Solution. Mitigation: establish clear ownership: Vehicle owns sensor data generation and basic hydraulic control; NGHE Solution owns productivity measurement and optimization.

Risk: If Safety Services interlocks are tuned per-site, there may be coupling between Safety Services and productivity KPI. Mitigation: document safety interlock thresholds as configuration parameters, not hard-coded logic.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm that NGHE Solution IM includes or owns the cloud analytics pipeline for productivity measurement. If analytics are external, define a clear contract for Telemetry interface (data schema, frequency, latency). Clarify ownership of productivity optimization logic (e.g., cycle time tuning) between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (integrated vehicle with optimized hydraulic cycle, real-time telemetry, and safety interlocks) directly addresses FR-NGHE-001 (achieve productivity target). The DP is decoupled: vehicle optimization, telemetry, and safety services are independent concerns. The DP is implementable with existing technology (hydraulic optimization, onboard sensors, cloud analytics). No functional gaps or contradictions identified.

diffusion_risks: Moderate diffusion risk: Productivity measurement logic is split between Vehicle (sensor data generation), NGHE Solution (aggregation and analytics), and potentially external cloud backend. Mitigation: establish clear ownership and interface contracts.
Low diffusion risk: Telemetry and Safety Services both use Network Link interface. Mitigation: design Network Link to support multiplexing or separate logical channels.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary. It owns the productivity KPI and integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services as sub-systems. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and do not create ambiguity about module boundaries.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via representative operations trial. Productivity metrics can be measured from onboard sensors and cloud analytics. Safety compliance can be verified by logging safety alerts and interlock activations. Acceptance criteria (productivity ≥ target, safety plan compliance) are measurable and time-bound.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns the productivity KPI. Sub-allocations to Vehicle (sensor data, hydraulic cycle), Teleoperation (operator interface), and Safety Services (policy enforcement) are well-defined and do not overlap.

recommended_revisions: Clarify the scope of cloud analytics: is it part of NGHE Solution or external? If external, define Telemetry interface contract (data schema, frequency, latency, SLA).
Define productivity measurement accuracy requirements (e.g., ±5%) and sensor calibration protocol.
Establish safety interlock tuning process to ensure interlocks do not excessively constrain productivity.
Document assumptions about network availability and latency for telemetry and tasking.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that is not atomic. It requires decomposition into sub-FRs that address distinct concerns: vehicle task execution, teleoperation capability, safety services, and productivity measurement. The refined FR and sub-FRs are well-scoped and ready for the next decomposition level. DP-A is sound and decoupled. NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary. Proceed to decompose sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) at the next level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and payload capacity.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote operator to command and monitor vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient for safe, productive task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and prevent unsafe task sequences.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The solution shall measure and report productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h) in real-time and post-operation.

assumptions: Onboard sensors (flow meters, pressure transducers, GNSS, load cells) are sufficiently accurate (±5%) and reliable over 8-hour shifts.
Cloud analytics pipeline can ingest and process telemetry with <5 min latency.
Worksite safety plan is well-defined and can be encoded as safety interlock rules.
Productivity target is customer-defined and achievable with optimized vehicle hydraulics and safe operation.
Network connectivity is available at the worksite with sufficient bandwidth for telemetry and tasking.
open_risks: Sensor calibration drift may degrade productivity measurement accuracy over time.
Network outages may interrupt telemetry and tasking; local buffering and post-sync required.
Safety interlocks may reduce achievable cycle time if overly conservative; field tuning required.
Productivity measurement logic may be diffused across Vehicle, NGHE Solution, and external cloud backend; ownership must be clarified.
Teleoperation latency and fidelity requirements are not yet quantified; must be defined in FR-NGHE-001.2 decomposition.
evidence_needed: Sensor accuracy and reliability data from field trials or vendor specifications.
Cloud analytics performance benchmarks (latency, throughput, availability).
Worksite safety plan documentation and operational policies.
Customer-defined productivity target and evaluation period.
Network availability and bandwidth assessment for the worksite.
Teleoperation latency and fidelity requirements from operator feedback or industry standards.
